content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>幫我確認</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-22</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-22T09:54:10.683Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\dream-config.ts\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\dream-config.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * dream-config — loader for ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/dream.json.\\n *\\n * Keeps the /dream meta-command decoupled from hard-coded step lists.\\n * Users edit JSON + /restart; bridge code doesn't change.\\n *\\n * Schema:\\n *   {\\n *     \\\"steps\\\": [\\n *       { \\\"cmd\\\": \\\"memorytoskill\\\", \\\"label\\\": \\\"...\\\" },\\n *       ...\\n *     ],\\n *     \\\"continue_on_error\\\": true\\n *   }\\n *\\n * Fallback: when the file is missing / invalid / empty, loader returns the\\n * DEFAULT_STEPS (the original four-step dream routine) so the bridge still\\n * works without any config file.\\n *\\n * Safety:\\n *   - validateStep() rejects anything that isn't a plain object with a\\n *     lowercase-alphanum `cmd` string — stops typos and type confusion.\\n *   - isReservedCmd() refuses `dream` itself + the meta-command's\\n *     aliases, preventing infinite recursion when a user lists\\n *     `/dream` inside its own step list.\\n *   - Handler existence (is `cmd` actually registered in COMMAND_HANDLERS?)\\n *     is deliberately deferred to handleDream() at run time, because\\n *     this module can't see the handler map without creating a circular\\n *     import with src/index.ts.\\n */\\n\\nimport { readFileSync, existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport { join } from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { detectAgentConfigDir } from \\\"./memory.js\\\";\\n\\nexport interface DreamStep {\\n  /** COMMAND_HANDLERS key (lowercase alphanum, no leading slash). */\\n  cmd: string;\\n  /** Optional display-only label. Shown alongside `/${cmd}` on Telegram. */\\n  label?: string;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface DreamConfig {\\n  steps: DreamStep[];\\n  continue_on_error: boolean;\\n  /** \\\"file\\\" when loaded from JSON; \\\"default\\\" when fallback was used. */\\n  source: \\\"file\\\" | \\\"default\\\";\\n  /** Resolved absolute path of the config file (exists or not). */\\n  configPath: string;\\n  /** Human-readable notes surfaced to the handler so it can warn the user. */\\n  warnings: string[];\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Built-in fallback — the full 10-step nightly routine. Used when\\n * dream.json is missing or malformed.\\n */\\nexport const DEFAULT_STEPS: readonly DreamStep[] = [\\n  { cmd: \\\"dailylog\\\",        label: \\\"產出今日 daily log 摘要\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"memorytoskill\\\", label: \\\"萃取 session → skill\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"topicreview\\\",   label: \\\"topic shard 重整\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"wikisync\\\",      label: \\\"從 topic shards 產出/更新 wiki pages\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"factlint\\\",      label: \\\"掃描矛盾/過時 facts 並清理\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"wikilint\\\",      label: \\\"掃描 wiki pages 健康狀態\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"skilllint\\\",     label: \\\"掃描 skills 健康狀態\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"docupdate\\\",    label: \\\"檢查並更新 usage-guide.html\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"specialistreview\\\", label: \\\"偵測未覆蓋 topic cluster 提議新 specialist\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"artifactcleanup\\\", label: \\\"清理超過 7 天的 artifact 檔案\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"backup\\\",        label: \\\"備份 AIMemory + agent config\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"restart\\\",       label: \\\"重啟 bridge（讓新 preamble 生效）\\\" },\\n] as const;\\n\\n/**\\n * The meta-command's own name. Anything in this set is rejected from step\\n * lists so a bad config can't cause dream to recurse into itself.\\n */\\nconst RESERVED_CMDS = new Set<string>([\\n  \\\"dream\\\",\\n]);\\n\\n/** Exported for smoke tests. */\\nexport function isReservedCmd(cmd: string): boolean {\\n  return RESERVED_CMDS.has(cmd.toLowerCase());\\n}\\n\\n/** Exported for smoke tests. */\\nexport function validateStep(s: unknown): s is DreamStep {\\n  if (!s || typeof s !== \\\"object\\\") return false;\\n  const obj = s as Record<string, unknown>;\\n  if (typeof obj.cmd !== \\\"string\\\") return false;\\n  // Command names: lowercase letters + digits. Same charset as\\n  // COMMAND_HANDLERS keys. Rejects whitespace, slashes, caps.\\n  if (!/^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$/.test(obj.cmd)) return false;\\n  if (obj.label !== undefined && typeof obj.label !== \\\"string\\\") return false;\\n  return true;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Normalise a raw parsed JSON payload into a DreamConfig.\\n * Exported so smoke tests can exercise the logic without touching disk.\\n *\\n * @param raw - JSON.parse output (any shape).\\n * @param configPath - passed through into the result for error messages.\\n */\\nexport function normaliseConfig(\\n  raw: unknown,\\n  configPath: string,\\n): DreamConfig {\\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\\n  const fallback: DreamConfig = {\\n    steps: [...DEFAULT_STEPS],\\n    continue_on_error: true,\\n    source: \\\"default\\\",\\n    configPath,\\n    warnings,\\n  };\\n\\n  if (!raw || typeof raw !== \\\"object\\\") {\\n    warnings.push(\\\"config 不是物件，使用 default\\\");\\n    return fallback;\\n  }\\n  const obj = raw as Record<string, unknown>;\\n\\n  if (!Array.isArray(obj.steps)) {\\n    warnings.push(\\\"config.steps 不是陣列，使用 default\\\");\\n    return fallback;\\n  }\\n\\n  const steps: DreamStep[] = [];\\n  for (let i = 0; i < obj.steps.length; i++) {\\n    const s = obj.steps[i];\\n    if (!validateStep(s)) {\\n      warnings.push(`steps[${i}] 格式錯誤，已跳過`);\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    if (isReservedCmd(s.cmd)) {\\n      warnings.push(`steps[${i}] cmd=\\\"${s.cmd}\\\" 為保留指令（會造成遞迴），已跳過`);\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    steps.push({ cmd: s.cmd.toLowerCase(), label: s.label });\\n  }\\n\\n  if (steps.length === 0) {\\n    warnings.push(\\\"config 沒有有效 steps，使用 default\\\");\\n    return fallback;\\n  }\\n\\n  // continue_on_error: default true when absent, false only when explicitly false.\\n  const continueOnError = obj.continue_on_error === false ? false : true;\\n\\n  return {\\n    steps,\\n    continue_on_error: continueOnError,\\n    source: \\\"file\\\",\\n    configPath,\\n    warnings,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Load ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/dream.json, falling back to DEFAULT_STEPS\\n * on any failure. Never throws — callers can rely on a usable result.\\n */\\nexport function loadDream(): DreamConfig {\\n  const agentDir = detectAgentConfigDir();\\n  const configPath = join(agentDir, \\\"dream.json\\\");\\n\\n  if (!existsSync(configPath)) {\\n    return {\\n      steps: [...DEFAULT_STEPS],\\n      continue_on_error: true,\\n      source: \\\"default\\\",\\n      configPath,\\n      warnings: [\\\"dream.json 不存在，使用 default\\\"],\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  let raw: unknown;\\n  try {\\n    const text = readFileSync(configPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    raw = JSON.parse(text);\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    return {\\n      steps: [...DEFAULT_STEPS],\\n      continue_on_error: true,\\n      source: \\\"default\\\",\\n      configPath,\\n      warnings: [\\n        `dream.json 讀取失敗：${(err as Error).message}，使用 default`,\\n      ],\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  return normaliseConfig(raw, configPath);\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":196,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":196}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Bash</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-22T09:54:14.045Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"command\":\"echo \\\"=== 使用者是否有自訂 dream.json 覆蓋預設步驟? ===\\\"\\nfor d in /c/Users/jiunchiwang/.kiro /c/Users/jiunchiwang/.codex /c/Users/jiunchiwang/.claude; do\\n  f=\\\"$d/dream.json\\\"\\n  if [ -f \\\"$f\\\" ]; then echo \\\"FOUND: $f\\\"; cat \\\"$f\\\"; else echo \\\"(無 $f)\\\"; fi\\ndone\",\"description\":\"Read default dream steps and check for user override\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"stdout\":\"=== 使用者是否有自訂 dream.json 覆蓋預設步驟? ===\\n(無 /c/Users/jiunchiwang/.kiro/dream.json)\\n(無 /c/Users/jiunchiwang/.codex/dream.json)\\n(無 /c/Users/jiunchiwang/.claude/dream.json)\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\dream-config.ts\",\"content\":\"/**\\n * dream-config — loader for ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/dream.json.\\n *\\n * Keeps the /dream meta-command decoupled from hard-coded step lists.\\n * Users edit JSON + /restart; bridge code doesn't change.\\n *\\n * Schema:\\n *   {\\n *     \\\"steps\\\": [\\n *       { \\\"cmd\\\": \\\"memorytoskill\\\", \\\"label\\\": \\\"...\\\" },\\n *       ...\\n *     ],\\n *     \\\"continue_on_error\\\": true\\n *   }\\n *\\n * Fallback: when the file is missing / invalid / empty, loader returns the\\n * DEFAULT_STEPS (the original four-step dream routine) so the bridge still\\n * works without any config file.\\n *\\n * Safety:\\n *   - validateStep() rejects anything that isn't a plain object with a\\n *     lowercase-alphanum `cmd` string — stops typos and type confusion.\\n *   - isReservedCmd() refuses `dream` itself + the meta-command's\\n *     aliases, preventing infinite recursion when a user lists\\n *     `/dream` inside its own step list.\\n *   - Handler existence (is `cmd` actually registered in COMMAND_HANDLERS?)\\n *     is deliberately deferred to handleDream() at run time, because\\n *     this module can't see the handler map without creating a circular\\n *     import with src/index.ts.\\n */\\n\\nimport { readFileSync, existsSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport { join } from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { detectAgentConfigDir } from \\\"./memory.js\\\";\\n\\nexport interface DreamStep {\\n  /** COMMAND_HANDLERS key (lowercase alphanum, no leading slash). */\\n  cmd: string;\\n  /** Optional display-only label. Shown alongside `/${cmd}` on Telegram. */\\n  label?: string;\\n}\\n\\nexport interface DreamConfig {\\n  steps: DreamStep[];\\n  continue_on_error: boolean;\\n  /** \\\"file\\\" when loaded from JSON; \\\"default\\\" when fallback was used. */\\n  source: \\\"file\\\" | \\\"default\\\";\\n  /** Resolved absolute path of the config file (exists or not). */\\n  configPath: string;\\n  /** Human-readable notes surfaced to the handler so it can warn the user. */\\n  warnings: string[];\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Built-in fallback — the full 10-step nightly routine. Used when\\n * dream.json is missing or malformed.\\n */\\nexport const DEFAULT_STEPS: readonly DreamStep[] = [\\n  { cmd: \\\"dailylog\\\",        label: \\\"產出今日 daily log 摘要\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"memorytoskill\\\", label: \\\"萃取 session → skill\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"topicreview\\\",   label: \\\"topic shard 重整\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"wikisync\\\",      label: \\\"從 topic shards 產出/更新 wiki pages\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"factlint\\\",      label: \\\"掃描矛盾/過時 facts 並清理\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"wikilint\\\",      label: \\\"掃描 wiki pages 健康狀態\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"skilllint\\\",     label: \\\"掃描 skills 健康狀態\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"docupdate\\\",    label: \\\"檢查並更新 usage-guide.html\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"specialistreview\\\", label: \\\"偵測未覆蓋 topic cluster 提議新 specialist\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"artifactcleanup\\\", label: \\\"清理超過 7 天的 artifact 檔案\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"backup\\\",        label: \\\"備份 AIMemory + agent config\\\" },\\n  { cmd: \\\"restart\\\",       label: \\\"重啟 bridge（讓新 preamble 生效）\\\" },\\n] as const;\\n\\n/**\\n * The meta-command's own name. Anything in this set is rejected from step\\n * lists so a bad config can't cause dream to recurse into itself.\\n */\\nconst RESERVED_CMDS = new Set<string>([\\n  \\\"dream\\\",\\n]);\\n\\n/** Exported for smoke tests. */\\nexport function isReservedCmd(cmd: string): boolean {\\n  return RESERVED_CMDS.has(cmd.toLowerCase());\\n}\\n\\n/** Exported for smoke tests. */\\nexport function validateStep(s: unknown): s is DreamStep {\\n  if (!s || typeof s !== \\\"object\\\") return false;\\n  const obj = s as Record<string, unknown>;\\n  if (typeof obj.cmd !== \\\"string\\\") return false;\\n  // Command names: lowercase letters + digits. Same charset as\\n  // COMMAND_HANDLERS keys. Rejects whitespace, slashes, caps.\\n  if (!/^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$/.test(obj.cmd)) return false;\\n  if (obj.label !== undefined && typeof obj.label !== \\\"string\\\") return false;\\n  return true;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Normalise a raw parsed JSON payload into a DreamConfig.\\n * Exported so smoke tests can exercise the logic without touching disk.\\n *\\n * @param raw - JSON.parse output (any shape).\\n * @param configPath - passed through into the result for error messages.\\n */\\nexport function normaliseConfig(\\n  raw: unknown,\\n  configPath: string,\\n): DreamConfig {\\n  const warnings: string[] = [];\\n  const fallback: DreamConfig = {\\n    steps: [...DEFAULT_STEPS],\\n    continue_on_error: true,\\n    source: \\\"default\\\",\\n    configPath,\\n    warnings,\\n  };\\n\\n  if (!raw || typeof raw !== \\\"object\\\") {\\n    warnings.push(\\\"config 不是物件，使用 default\\\");\\n    return fallback;\\n  }\\n  const obj = raw as Record<string, unknown>;\\n\\n  if (!Array.isArray(obj.steps)) {\\n    warnings.push(\\\"config.steps 不是陣列，使用 default\\\");\\n    return fallback;\\n  }\\n\\n  const steps: DreamStep[] = [];\\n  for (let i = 0; i < obj.steps.length; i++) {\\n    const s = obj.steps[i];\\n    if (!validateStep(s)) {\\n      warnings.push(`steps[${i}] 格式錯誤，已跳過`);\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    if (isReservedCmd(s.cmd)) {\\n      warnings.push(`steps[${i}] cmd=\\\"${s.cmd}\\\" 為保留指令（會造成遞迴），已跳過`);\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    steps.push({ cmd: s.cmd.toLowerCase(), label: s.label });\\n  }\\n\\n  if (steps.length === 0) {\\n    warnings.push(\\\"config 沒有有效 steps，使用 default\\\");\\n    return fallback;\\n  }\\n\\n  // continue_on_error: default true when absent, false only when explicitly false.\\n  const continueOnError = obj.continue_on_error === false ? false : true;\\n\\n  return {\\n    steps,\\n    continue_on_error: continueOnError,\\n    source: \\\"file\\\",\\n    configPath,\\n    warnings,\\n  };\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Load ${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/dream.json, falling back to DEFAULT_STEPS\\n * on any failure. Never throws — callers can rely on a usable result.\\n */\\nexport function loadDream(): DreamConfig {\\n  const agentDir = detectAgentConfigDir();\\n  const configPath = join(agentDir, \\\"dream.json\\\");\\n\\n  if (!existsSync(configPath)) {\\n    return {\\n      steps: [...DEFAULT_STEPS],\\n      continue_on_error: true,\\n      source: \\\"default\\\",\\n      configPath,\\n      warnings: [\\\"dream.json 不存在，使用 default\\\"],\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  let raw: unknown;\\n  try {\\n    const text = readFileSync(configPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n    raw = JSON.parse(text);\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    return {\\n      steps: [...DEFAULT_STEPS],\\n      continue_on_error: true,\\n      source: \\\"default\\\",\\n      configPath,\\n      warnings: [\\n        `dream.json 讀取失敗：${(err as Error).message}，使用 default`,\\n      ],\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  return normaliseConfig(raw, configPath);\\n}\\n\",\"numLines\":196,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":196}}"</outcome>

content:
PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
完整確認了。結論很乾淨:

## `.usage.json` 檢查結果

**1. 沒有重複計數 ✓**
只有一個 `ui-ux-pro-max` key(出現次數 = 1)。「steering 那份」和「skills 那份」沒有被當成兩個 skill 重複統計 — 因為 bridge 的 usage key 只用 skill 名,不含路徑。

**2. 但這筆 `orphan: true` 是過時的 — 而且再次佐證根因**

那筆內容:
```json
"ui-ux-pro-max": {
  "created_at": "2026-06-17T...", "use_count": 1,
  "last_reason": "生成…設計系統(色彩、字型、元件建議)",
  "orphan": true, "pinned": false
}
```

`orphan: true` 的意思是「bridge 在 `${SKILL_DIR}`(=`~/.kiro/skills/`)掃不到這個 SKILL.md」。但現在 junction 明明在、SKILL.md 讀得到 — 所以這個標記**現在是錯的**。

為什麼會這樣?時間線再次對上:
- **六月 17** agent 用了一次 ui-ux-pro-max → `markUsed()` 在寫入時 `stat` 一下 `~/.kiro/skills/ui-ux-pro-max/SKILL.md`(`usageStore.ts:229`),那時它還躺在 **steering**、不在 skills/ → 標 `orphan: true`。
- **六月 19** canonical 遷移 + junction 才讓它正式出現在 skills/,但 orphan 旗標沒人回頭清。

換句話說,這個 orphan 旗標本身就是「六月中它被放錯在 steering」的獨立鐵證。

**3. 會自己修好 — 不用手動改**

`migrateFromDisk()`(`usageStore.ts:168-169`)掃到 SKILL.md 重新出現時會自動清 orphan:
```ts
} else if (next.skills[name].orphan) {
  next.skills[name] = { ...next.skills[name], orphan: false };  // de-orphan
}
```
這支由 `/skillusage` 觸發(`misc.ts:483`),而 `/skillusage`...等等,實際上 `/dream` 的步驟裡有 `skilllint`/`skillusage` 類掃描。**下次跑 `/skillusage`(或夜間 `/dream`)就會自動把它 de-orphan**,還會在報告列「de-orphaned names (SKILL.md reappeared)」。

## 我的建議:不要手動改 json

bridge 自己會 reconcile,手動編輯反而可能跟它的 sweep 邏輯打架。你只要在 Telegram 跑一次 `/skillusage`,這筆就會自動轉成 `orphan: false`,整個 ui-ux-pro-max 的狀態就完全乾淨了。

要的話我可以幫你確認 `/dream` 的預設步驟裡有沒有涵蓋這個 sweep(這樣你連手動都不用,夜間排程會自動清)。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

  <what_happened>Bash</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-22T09:54:39.844Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"command\":\"cd /g/AI/telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\necho \\\"=== skilllint handler 是哪支?有沒有呼叫 migrateFromDisk / listAll(會掃磁碟 de-orphan) ===\\\"\\ngrep -rn \\\"skilllint\\\\|skillusage\\\" src/index.ts src/commands/*.ts 2>/dev/null | grep -i \\\"command\\\\|HANDLERS\\\\|=>\\\\|handle\\\" | head\\necho \\\"\\\"\\necho \\\"=== migrateFromDisk 在哪些 handler 被呼叫(這支才會 de-orphan) ===\\\"\\ngrep -rn \\\"migrateFromDisk\\\" src/ 2>/dev/null\\necho \\\"\\\"\\necho \\\"=== markListed 會不會 de-orphan? 看 usageStore.ts:189-218 ===\\\"\\nsed -n '189,260p' src/usageStore.ts\",\"description\":\"Trace skilllint vs skillusage and de-orphan triggers\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"stdout\":\"=== skilllint handler 是哪支?有沒有呼叫 migrateFromDisk / listAll(會掃磁碟 de-orphan) ===\\nsrc/index.ts:492:  skilllint: handleSkillLint,\\nsrc/index.ts:499:  skillusage: handleSkillUsage,\\nsrc/index.ts:589:bot.command(\\\"skilllint\\\", handleSkillLint);\\nsrc/index.ts:599:bot.command(\\\"skillusage\\\", handleSkillUsage);\\nsrc/commands/memory.ts:417:// ─── /skilllint ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\nsrc/commands/misc.ts:470:// ─── /skillusage ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n=== migrateFromDisk 在哪些 handler 被呼叫(這支才會 de-orphan) ===\\nsrc/commands/misc.ts:483:    sweep = await usageStore.migrateFromDisk(skillDir);\\nsrc/usageStore.ts:8://   • migrateFromDisk() — on first run (and occasionally thereafter),\\nsrc/usageStore.ts:139:export async function migrateFromDisk(\\n\\n=== markListed 會不會 de-orphan? 看 usageStore.ts:189-218 ===\\nexport async function markListed(\\n  skillDir: string,\\n  name: string,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  const ts = nowIso();\\n  await updateJson<UsageJson>(\\n    usageFilePath(skillDir),\\n    emptyUsageJson,\\n    (current) => {\\n      const next: UsageJson = {\\n        ...current,\\n        _last_updated_at: ts,\\n        skills: { ...current.skills },\\n      };\\n      const existing = next.skills[name];\\n      if (existing) {\\n        next.skills[name] = { ...existing, last_bridge_listed_at: ts };\\n      } else {\\n        next.skills[name] = {\\n          ...newEntry(ts, /* orphan */ true),\\n          last_bridge_listed_at: ts,\\n        };\\n      }\\n      return next;\\n    },\\n  );\\n}\\n\\n/** Record that the agent used this skill (from a <<SKILL_USED>> token).\\n * Creates an orphan entry if `name` isn't on disk. */\\nexport async function markUsed(\\n  skillDir: string,\\n  name: string,\\n  reason: string,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  const ts = nowIso();\\n  // Detect orphan at write time. We do one `stat` per call — cheap and\\n  // avoids stale in-memory caches disagreeing with disk.\\n  let diskExists = false;\\n  try {\\n    const st = await stat(path.join(skillDir, name, \\\"SKILL.md\\\"));\\n    diskExists = st.isFile();\\n  } catch {\\n    diskExists = false;\\n  }\\n\\n  await updateJson<UsageJson>(\\n    usageFilePath(skillDir),\\n    emptyUsageJson,\\n    (current) => {\\n      const next: UsageJson = {\\n        ...current,\\n        _last_updated_at: ts,\\n        skills: { ...current.skills },\\n      };\\n      const existing = next.skills[name];\\n      if (existing) {\\n        next.skills[name] = {\\n          ...existing,\\n          last_agent_used_at: ts,\\n          last_reason: reason,\\n          use_count: existing.use_count + 1,\\n          // Transition in/out of orphan state based on current disk.\\n          orphan: !diskExists,\\n        };\\n      } else {\\n        next.skills[name] = {\\n          ...newEntry(ts, !diskExists),\\n          last_agent_used_at: ts,\\n          last_reason: reason,\\n          use_count: 1,\\n        };\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
The user in the primary session asked "幫我確認" (Help me confirm/verify) in Chinese. The primary Claude then:

1. Read the file `dream-config.ts` which contains configuration loader logic for a `/dream` meta-command
2. Checked if there's a user-customized `dream.json` file in any of the three possible agent config directories (.kiro, .codex, .claude)

Result: No custom dream.json exists, so the system will use the DEFAULT_STEPS.

Looking at the code:
- This is a Telegram-Kiro bridge system
- The dream-config.ts file defines a loader for dream.json configuration
- It has a DEFAULT_STEPS array with 12 steps for a nightly routine (dailylog, memorytoskill, topicreview, wikisync, factlint, wikilint, skilllint, docupdate, specialistreview, artifactcleanup, backup, restart)
- The system checks three directories for a user override: ~/.kiro, ~/.codex, ~/.claude
- No override was found

This is a "discovery" type observation - the primary session is learning about the existing system state (what dream steps are configured).

Let me create an observation:
- Type: discovery (learning about existing configuration)
- Title: Dream command configuration verified
- Subtitle: No custom dream.json found; system uses default 12-step nightly routine
- Facts: 
  - dream-config.ts defines a 12-step default routine (dailylog, memorytoskill, topicreview, wikisync, factlint, wikilint, skilllint, docupdate, specialistreview, artifactcleanup, backup, restart)
  - System checks three directories for dream.json override: ~/.kiro, ~/.codex, ~/.claude
  - No custom dream.json exists in any agent config directory
- Narrative: Verified the dream command configuration for the Telegram-Kiro bridge. 

The bridge will fall back to executing the default 12-step nightly routine since no custom configuration overrides it. This includes memory extraction, topic review, wiki synchronization, linting checks across health/wiki/skills, documentation updates, specialist review, artifact cleanup, backup, and restart operations.

- Concepts: how-it-works, why-it-exists

, problem-solution, gotcha, pattern, trade-off
  This demonstrates how-it-works (the config loading mechanism with fallback behavior) and pattern (the override-with-fallback approach)
- Files read: src/dream-config.ts
- Files modified: none

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Dream command configuration verified</title>
  <subtitle>No custom dream.json found; system uses default 12-step nightly routine</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>dream-config.ts defines DEFAULT_STEPS with 12 steps: dailylog, memorytoskill, topicreview, wikisync, factlint, wikilint, skilllint, docupdate, specialistreview, artifactcleanup, backup, restart</fact>
    <fact>Configuration loader checks three directories for dream.json override: ~/.kiro, ~/.codex, ~/.claude</fact>
    <fact>No custom dream.json exists in any agent config directory; system falls back to DEFAULT_STEPS</fact>
    <fact>loadDream() function returns default 12-step routine with continue_on_error: true and source: "default"</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Verified the dream command configuration for the Telegram-Kiro bridge system. The dream-config.ts loader attempts to read dream.json from agent config directories (.kiro, .codex, or .claude) to allow users to customize the nightly routine steps without modifying code. When no custom config exists, it falls back to DEFAULT_STEPS—a 12-step routine covering daily logging, memory extraction, topic review, wiki synchronization, health linting (facts/wiki/skills), documentation updates, specialist reviews, artifact cleanup, backup, and bridge restart. Inspection confirmed no user override is present, so the bridge executes the full default routine. The loader includes validation to reject malformed steps and reserved commands that would cause recursion.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/dream-config.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

We do one `stat` per call — cheap and\\n  // avoids stale in-memory caches disagreeing with disk.\\n  let diskExists = false;\\n  try {\\n    const st = await stat(path.join(skillDir, name, \\\"SKILL.md\\\"));\\n    diskExists = st.isFile();\\n  } catch {\\n    diskExists = false;\\n  }\\n\\n  await updateJson<UsageJson>(\\n    usageFilePath(skillDir),\\n    emptyUsageJson,\\n    (current) => {\\n      const next: UsageJson = {\\n        ...current,\\n        _last_updated_at: ts,\\n        skills: { ...current.skills },\\n      };\\n      const existing = next.skills[name];\\n      if (existing) {\\n        next.skills[name] = {\\n          ...existing,\\n          last_agent_used_at: ts,\\n          last_reason: reason,\\n          use_count: existing.use_count + 1,\\n          // Transition in/out of orphan state based on current disk.\\n          orphan: !diskExists,\\n        };\\n      } else {\\n        next.skills[name] = {\\n          ...newEntry(ts, !diskExists),\\n          last_agent_used_at: ts,\\n          last_reason: reason,\\n          use_count: 1,\\n        };\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>

```xml
<summary>
  <request>Verify skill usage tracking status and orphan flag for ui-ux-pro-max skill</request>
  <investigated>Examined dream-config.ts to understand default nightly routine steps. Checked for custom dream.json overrides in agent config directories (~/.kiro, ~/.codex, ~/.claude). Traced skilllint and skillusage command handlers to understand de-orphaning triggers. Analyzed usageStore.ts functions (migrateFromDisk, markListed, markUsed) to confirm how orphan flags are set and cleared.</investigated>
  <learned>The ui-ux-pro-max skill shows orphan: true in .usage.json because it was used on June 17 when still located in the steering directory, before the June 19 canonical migration and junction creation moved it to skills/. The markUsed() function checks disk at write time and sets orphan: true when SKILL.md isn't found in ~/.kiro/skills/. The migrateFromDisk() function auto-clears orphan flags when SKILL.md reappears, triggered by /skillusage command (misc.ts:483). No duplicate counting exists—usage tracking keys on skill name only, not path. The default dream routine includes 12 steps: dailylog, memorytoskill, topicreview, wikisync, factlint, wikilint, skilllint, docupdate, specialistreview, artifactcleanup, backup, restart.</learned>
  <completed>Confirmed no custom dream.json override exists—system uses DEFAULT_STEPS from dream-config.ts. Verified the orphan flag will auto-correct on next /skillusage run without manual JSON editing. Traced the de-orphaning code path through usageStore.ts functions.</completed>
  <next_steps>Confirming whether the default /dream routine includes skill usage sweeps that would automatically de-orphan ui-ux-pro-max during nightly runs, eliminating need for manual intervention.</next_steps>
  <notes>The outdated orphan: true flag serves as independent evidence of the June 17-19 migration timeline—the skill was inaccessible in ~/.kiro/skills/ when first used, then became accessible after junction creation. The bridge's reconciliation logic is designed to self-heal this state without manual JSON editing.</notes>
</summary>
```
